preferred_outline.description: Event/date/time table for the Hong Kong Public Offering and listing.
Hong Kong time convention, change notice, severe weather or extreme conditions if supported.

style_rules: Use a table. Do not write promotional prose.
Keep every standard timetable event and mark unsupported dates as DATA_MISSING.

preferred_outline.description: Exact prospectus chapter order with page placeholders if pagination is unknown.

style_rules: Use generated section titles exactly. Do not add chapters that are not generated.

preferred_outline.description: Balanced issuer overview and offering context.
Revenue model and monetisation only if supported.
Neutral strengths only when evidenced by source documents.
Current plans only; no certainty or forecast.
Concise historical financial tables where data exists.
Short cross-reference to principal risks.
Offer size, price range and use of proceeds only if supported.

style_rules: Use the prospectus opening caution that the summary should be read with the whole document.
Market ranks, shares and CAGR must carry AI:CITE tags or be omitted.
forbidden_patterns: new risk themes not in Risk Factors

preferred_outline.description: Alphabetical dictionary of defined terms.

style_rules: Use a two-column term/meaning table or concise dictionary list.
Do not define terms not used elsewhere or not supported by source documents.

preferred_outline.description: Alphabetical technical terms relevant to the issuer's business.

style_rules: Plain-language explanations only; keep legal/entity definitions in Definitions.

preferred_outline.description: Safe-harbour style cautionary language and examples of forward-looking terminology.

style_rules: Use standard cautionary prose. Keep it generic unless company-specific supported factors are provided.

preferred_outline.description: Industry-specific risks with one risk per heading.
Operations, customers, suppliers, technology, IP, data and employees.

preferred_outline.subsection: Risks Relating to Our Financial Condition
preferred_outline.description: Historical losses, liquidity, receivables, fair value and working capital where supported.

preferred_outline.subsection: Risks Relating to Laws and Regulations
preferred_outline.description: Licences, cybersecurity, data, foreign investment, VIE or overseas listing risks where supported.

preferred_outline.subsection: Risks Relating to the Global Offering
preferred_outline.description: Trading, liquidity, dilution, WVR and offering mechanics risks where supported.

style_rules: Use real prospectus risk style: heading states the risk, followed by paragraphs explaining cause and adverse effect.
Do not use Factual Cause/Harm bullet templates in final prose.
No mitigation language in the same risk narrative.
source_priorities: risk_seeds.items and sponsor due-diligence/risk memos
financials and operating metrics that show concentration, losses, liquidity, receivables or working-capital pressure
business_products, customers_suppliers, rd_ip and regulatory_legal evidence for company-specific operational and compliance risks
offering_use_of_proceeds and share-capital data for offering/WVR/dilution/liquidity risks

required_content_slots.missing_action: include only generic industry-risk skeletons with DATA_MISSING and request industry consultant / sponsor risk input
do not invent operations; request business DD, customer/supplier schedules and technology/IP evidence
mark financial-risk analysis DATA_MISSING unless historical financial tables support it
request legal/regulatory memo; avoid counsel conclusions
keep standard offering-risk placeholders and cross-reference Underwriting/Global Offering Structure

prospectus_phrases: If any of the following risks actually occurs, our business, financial condition and results of operations could be materially and adversely affected.
We cannot assure you that...
Any failure to... may materially and adversely affect...
Our historical results may not be indicative of our future performance.
quality_checks: Each risk heading must itself state the risk, not merely name a topic.
Each risk narrative must include cause, issuer-specific context and adverse effect.
Do not mention mitigation or internal controls unless the risk is about failure of those controls.
Numerical concentration/financial risks must cite the supporting fact id/source.
missing_data_policy: Where a risk category has no issuer-specific evidence, keep the category heading only if standard practice requires it and insert DATA_MISSING with a concrete source request.
Do not create risk factors from general market knowledge.

preferred_outline.description: Each waiver by rule, reason, conditions and sponsor/counsel basis.

style_rules: Use checklist format when source data is thin.

preferred_outline.subsection: Information about this Prospectus and the Global Offering
preferred_outline.description: Responsibility, selling restrictions, application reliance and offer mechanics cautions.

style_rules: Use standard legal/prospectus cautionary language with placeholders for missing deal terms.

preferred_outline.description: Director names, addresses and responsibilities.

preferred_outline.subsection: Parties Involved in the Global Offering
preferred_outline.description: Sponsors, underwriters, legal advisers, auditors, industry consultant and receiving bank.

style_rules: Prefer tables. Do not invent advisers or addresses.

preferred_outline.description: Registered office, headquarters, principal place of business, company secretary, authorised representatives and websites.

style_rules: Use table format; mark unknown fields DATA_MISSING.

preferred_outline.description: Scope and jurisdictions of applicable regulation.
Material licences and approval status.
Restrictions and filings where applicable.

preferred_outline.subsection: Data Privacy, Cybersecurity and Content Compliance
preferred_outline.description: Data lifecycle, cybersecurity review, personal information and content obligations where relevant.
IP ownership, registration and enforcement regime.
Product, consumer, labour, tax or other material rules.

style_rules: For each topic state rule set, regulator, core requirement and issuer relevance/status only if supported.

preferred_outline.description: Third-party report and public source disclaimer.
Industry definition and value chain.
Historical and forecast market data with source/scope/date/metric.
Demand drivers, technology, regulation and adoption trends.
Ranking, market share and competition only with sourced support.
Technology, capital, data, customer or regulatory barriers where sourced.

style_rules: Every statistic, ranking, share, CAGR and forecast requires AI:CITE.
Do not turn industry analysis into issuer promotion.
forbidden_patterns: issuer strengths disguised as industry facts
source_priorities: industry_market.consultant_engagement, methodology, market_size, CAGR, ranking and market_share fields
independent consultant report excerpts, public-source extracts and source disclaimers
business_products only for defining issuer's market participation, not for proving market size

required_content_slots.missing_action: insert CONSULTANT_REPORT_REQUIRED and avoid all market statistics
use a short DATA_MISSING placeholder; do not define a new market from common knowledge
omit charts/tables and request consultant market-size data
state DATA_MISSING; do not turn issuer strategy into industry trend

required_content_slots.slot: Competitive landscape, ranking and market share
required_content_slots.missing_action: omit issuer rank/share claims and request consultant ranking table

table_patterns.name: Market size table
table_patterns.when: market_size or CAGR data is available

table_patterns.name: Competitive landscape table
table_patterns.when: ranking or market_share data is available

prospectus_phrases: According to the industry consultant report...
The market is expected to grow from... to...
The following table sets forth...
Key market drivers include...
quality_checks: Every statistic must carry source, scope, period and metric.
Do not describe the issuer as a leader unless ranking/share evidence exists.
Separate industry facts from company strengths.
missing_data_policy: If consultant data is missing, produce a source-request skeleton rather than a generic industry essay.

preferred_outline.description: Issuer history and corporate development.
Founding, financing, acquisitions, disposals and group development.
Pre-listing reorganization steps and rationale.
Group chart narrative, subsidiaries and VIE/controlled entities where applicable.
M&A, SAFE, CSRC or overseas listing filings where supported.

style_rules: Use chronological order and avoid unexplained entity jumps.

preferred_outline.description: Who the issuer is, what it sells, where it operates and key scale metrics.
Revenue model, pricing, delivery, monetisation and customer contracts.
Products, platforms or services grouped by segment, not endless lists.
R&D, technology architecture, IP and innovation system.
Customer base, key customers, sales process and concentration.
Material suppliers, procurement, production, delivery and operations.
Data lifecycle, controls, incidents and compliance if relevant.
Headcount, functions, qualifications and training.
Properties, insurance, claims, litigation and material compliance matters.

style_rules: Mission, vision, strengths and strategies are optional and may appear only if the source context contains those exact concepts.
Group product lists into categories when more than 10 items appear.
Use cross-references to Regulation and Risk Factors for risk-sensitive operational matters.
source_priorities: business_products narratives and structured facts
customers_suppliers schedules for customer base, sales channels, concentration, supplier and procurement discussion
rd_ip for technology, R&D, IP, data privacy and cybersecurity discussion
financials.segment_revenue and operating_metrics for scale and segment contribution
regulatory_legal only for compliance-sensitive cross-references

required_content_slots.missing_action: draft only a short overview with DATA_MISSING for scale metrics
request business model memo and revenue breakdown; do not infer monetisation

required_content_slots.slot: Products, platforms and services by segment
required_content_slots.missing_action: use segment placeholders and request product/service catalogue
request customer concentration and sales channel schedule

required_content_slots.slot: Suppliers, production, quality control and logistics
required_content_slots.missing_action: keep a DATA_MISSING operational subsection rather than inventing supply chain details

required_content_slots.slot: Technology, R&D, IP and data protection
required_content_slots.missing_action: request R&D/IP/data-security support and avoid technical superiority claims

required_content_slots.slot: Employees, properties, insurance, legal proceedings and compliance
required_content_slots.missing_action: include only if source data exists; otherwise keep a concise DATA_MISSING note

table_patterns.name: Top customers/suppliers concentration
table_patterns.when: customer or supplier concentration data exists

table_patterns.name: R&D and IP summary
table_patterns.when: R&D headcount, expenses or IP portfolio facts exist

prospectus_phrases: We are...
During the Track Record Period, we generated revenue primarily from...
The following table sets forth a breakdown of our revenue by...
As of the Latest Practicable Date...
quality_checks: The first two paragraphs must identify the issuer, business, geography and scale with evidence.
Each major product/service category should map to revenue or operating evidence where available.
Use 'we believe' sparingly and only for supported management statements.
Cross-reference risk-sensitive matters instead of burying caveats in promotional prose.
missing_data_policy: If only structured facts exist, draft table-heavy prose and request narrative DD material.
If product/customer/supplier/R&D slots are missing, preserve headings with DATA_MISSING rather than inventing operations.

preferred_outline.description: Why contractual arrangements are used and relevant operating entities.
Core VIE agreements and control/economic benefit mechanics.
Material limitations and PRC law uncertainty with cross-reference to Risk Factors.
Legal adviser views only if supported.

style_rules: If VIE is not applicable, generate a short not-applicable placeholder with verification tag.

preferred_outline.description: Identity, shareholding and control relationship.
Management, operational and financial independence.
Competing businesses and non-compete undertakings where supported.
Measures to manage conflicts.

style_rules: Do not assume independence or non-compete undertakings without evidence.

preferred_outline.description: Connected persons and transaction categories.
Agreements, parties, terms, caps and Listing Rules classification.
Waivers, annual caps and review procedures.
Views only where source support exists.

style_rules: Use tables for agreements/caps. Preserve missing caps as DATA_MISSING.

preferred_outline.description: Executive, non-executive and independent non-executive directors.
Senior managers and responsibilities.
Company secretary qualification and role.
Audit, remuneration, nomination and other committees.
Compliance, training, diversity and governance measures where supported.

style_rules: Use biographical prose only from supplied CV/source data.

preferred_outline.description: Shareholding table immediately following the Global Offering.

style_rules: Use table format and distinguish class, number and percentage.

preferred_outline.description: Authorised and issued shares, changes on completion of offering and assumptions.
Assumptions on over-allotment option, share incentive schemes and conversion if supported.

style_rules: Use tables and preserve assumptions. Do not calculate missing totals unless inputs are complete.

preferred_outline.description: Basis of presentation, track record period and IFRS/accountants' report reference.
Revenue sources and cost structure relevant to financial performance.

preferred_outline.subsection: Major Factors Affecting Our Results of Operations
preferred_outline.description: Company-specific and general factors.

preferred_outline.subsection: Description of Selected Components of Results of Operations
preferred_outline.description: Revenue, cost of sales, gross profit, expenses, other income/losses and tax.

preferred_outline.subsection: Period-to-Period Comparison of Results of Operations
preferred_outline.description: Historical comparison only.
Cash flow, working capital, indebtedness and capital commitments.
Receivables, inventories, financial assets, liabilities and equity.

preferred_outline.subsection: Key Financial Ratios and Non-IFRS Measures
preferred_outline.description: Only if defined and reconcilable.
Policies and judgments that materially affect financial statements.

style_rules: Historical analysis only. No future profit/margin forecast.
Use tables when multiple periods are available.
If a driver is unavailable, state DATA_MISSING instead of inventing management explanation.
source_priorities: financials.income_statement, balance_sheet, cash_flow and segment_revenue
financials.indebtedness, working-capital, commitments, receivables, inventories and liquidity facts
business_products.business_model and customers_suppliers only to explain historical drivers supported by figures
accountants' report / MD&A source documents where available

required_content_slots.slot: Basis of presentation and track record period
required_content_slots.missing_action: request accountants' report / financial statements

required_content_slots.slot: Selected consolidated statements of profit or loss
required_content_slots.missing_action: do not describe revenue/profit trends without income statement data

required_content_slots.slot: Revenue and cost breakdown by segment/period
required_content_slots.missing_action: request segment revenue/cost breakdown; avoid generic growth drivers
provide numeric comparison only and mark management explanation DATA_MISSING

required_content_slots.slot: Liquidity, cash flow, indebtedness and working capital
required_content_slots.missing_action: request cash flow, indebtedness and working-capital schedules

required_content_slots.slot: Selected balance sheet items and ageing
required_content_slots.missing_action: include only supported items; otherwise mark DATA_MISSING

prospectus_phrases: The following table sets forth selected items of our consolidated statements of profit or loss...
Our revenue increased/decreased primarily due to...
As at..., we had...
Net cash generated from/used in operating activities...
quality_checks: Every trend sentence must cite the figures it compares.
Do not explain a variance unless the cause exists in evidence.
No forecasts, target margins or future profitability claims.
Non-IFRS measures require definition, reconciliation and consistent naming.
missing_data_policy: When management explanation is absent, state DATA_MISSING after the numeric comparison.
When financial statements are absent, output only a source-request skeleton.

preferred_outline.description: Business plans tied to use of proceeds.
Allocation table by purpose, amount and percentage.

style_rules: Percentages must sum to 100 if percentages are used.
source_priorities: offering_use_of_proceeds.use_of_proceeds allocation schedule
offering_use_of_proceeds.offer for gross/net proceeds and offer-price assumptions
business_products strategies/future plans only when tied to proceeds allocation
underwriting/listing expense facts for deductions and net proceeds

required_content_slots.slot: Gross proceeds, net proceeds and offer-price assumption
required_content_slots.missing_action: request offer size/price/net proceeds assumptions
do not invent allocation percentages; mark COUNSEL_INPUT_REQUIRED
request implementation timeline and milestones for each allocation
insert standard placeholder only if counsel confirms treasury/deposit treatment

prospectus_phrases: Assuming an Offer Price of...
We estimate that we will receive net proceeds of approximately...
We intend to use the net proceeds for the following purposes...
To the extent that the net proceeds are not immediately used...
quality_checks: Percentages must sum to 100% unless the source explicitly says otherwise.
Every proceeds purpose must connect to a supported future plan or business need.
Do not create acquisition, R&D or marketing budgets without allocation evidence.
missing_data_policy: If allocation is missing, produce only the heading, DATA_MISSING and the exact source request.

preferred_outline.description: Hong Kong underwriting parties and agreement.
Obligations, commissions, expenses and termination.
Issuer/shareholder undertakings if supported.
Stabilisation and market-making where applicable.

style_rules: Use standard underwriting structure; keep deal-specific names/fees as DATA_MISSING if absent.

preferred_outline.description: Offer structure, public/international split and reallocation.
Price range, price determination and allocation basis.
Application and allocation mechanics.
Placing and over-allotment option if supported.
Stabilising manager and permitted activities if supported.

style_rules: Interlock with Expected Timetable, How to Apply and Underwriting.
